purpose: The commit fixes a potential use-after-free (UAF) issue by checking if HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set before queuing commands.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c since it's already included via linux/tty.h. This cleanup improves code clarity.
The commit updates documentation for a store metric 'N/A', adding explanations in two sections of the perf-c2c.txt file.

The commit fixes issues related to vma lock handling in hugetlb during VMA splitting and range unmapping by adjusting how locks are allocated and freed, preventing race conditions and ensuring proper resource management.
The commit updates device tree bindings for the realtek,rt5682s codec by adding AVDD and MICVDD supply definitions. It enhances documentation to ensure proper power management configuration.
Fixes a resource leak by adding iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one(), ensuring proper cleanup of mapped memory.
This commit removes an unused 'work' field from the mlx5e_async_ctx structure, cleaning up unnecessary code.
The commit changes an unsigned integer (u8) to a signed integer (s8) in a struct to handle negative values (-1) without causing static checker warnings.
This commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfig files to match savedefconfig output, ensuring consistency without functional changes.
Introduces a helper function to check CAN-FD mode, replacing repeated checks in multiple files for cleaner code.
The commit modifies test cases to skip execution when the RTC device is not present, preventing unnecessary test failures in environments without RTC hardware.
The commit removes a redundant variable assignment that was causing a clang warning about dead code. It improves code cleanliness by eliminating unused variables.
This commit modifies rcuscale to decouple it from RCU Tasks, reducing kernel size and complexity by conditionally including tasks-related code only when needed.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in a comment within spte.h. It corrects a typo in the documentation, improving clarity.
Reverts changes in clock table population to prevent underflow caused by incorrect dcfclk-based calculations.
Adds device tree bindings for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU by introducing a new compatible string.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files for consistency with other naming conventions in the DW PCIe core driver.
The commit fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in a debug message to prevent type mismatch issues.
Fixes a range check in MIPS jump labels to prevent warnings caused by negative offsets.
The commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls another function to improve code structure by eliminating the hal layer.
The commit modifies a symbol to be static to resolve a sparse warning, improving code quality.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from device tree bindings for display subsystems, correcting configuration issues.

The commit modifies how arrays are allocated in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic array size changes by drivers. It renames variables and functions related to dynamic arrays to a more general 'array' terminology.
Adds support for new PMICs (e.g., PM8350) and SoCs (e.g., SM8350) in the socinfo driver to recognize these devices.

The commit updates the RTL8xxxu Wi-Fi driver to correctly identify all possible chip cuts (versions) from A to P. Previously, only up to E was recognized, and higher values were labeled as 'unknown'. The change simplifies the code by replacing a switch statement with a direct calculation.
Removes redundant .is_enabled state and associated functions from PRCMU clocks, as the core already manages enable counts.
The commit reorganizes code to minimize #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage by grouping related cases and moving macro definitions.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree to prepare for adding I2C functionality later.
The commit improves comment clarity by correcting typos and rephrasing for better understanding.
Fixes an infinite loop in resource release by adding error handling when HW semaphore acquisition fails.

The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology by replacing 'master_mode' with 'provider_mode' and adjusting related variables and error messages. This change aligns the code with current standards without altering functionality.
The commit removes an unnecessary hack in NFS code related to IS_SWAPFILE, which is always defined. The removed lines were a temporary workaround for future swap support.

This commit implements handling for GTP Echo Request and Response messages by adding new functions to process these messages, ensuring compliance with GTP specifications. It introduces necessary structures and logic to handle both GTPv0 and GTPv1 echo responses.

This commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate and introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero(). The changes aim to improve code clarity and efficiency by using more suitable functions for specific use cases.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR as the firmware no longer supports it. This prevents errors when roaming between access points and stops console messages about the failure.
Adds a new board configuration for Intel's ADL platform with the RT5682 codec, supporting systems without a speaker amplifier.
The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg requests if the socket does not support zero-copy operations.
The commit modifies the documentation preamble to check for xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing errors when the package is missing despite having the CJK font.
The commit modifies checkpatch.pl to stop reporting errors for 'case' macros used in switch statements. It adds an exception to prevent false positives when such macros are defined.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers in the DRM driver for better code structure and sharing among drivers. No functional changes are made.
Adds support for multiple JPEG decoding hardware cores to enable parallel processing.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() debug statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb function, as their information is already covered by tracepoints.
This commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods to optimize power usage. It introduces new functions and debug options related to zstate optimization.
Fixes a CFI failure by correcting the type of reset_type parameter in hqd_destroy() callback to match its actual usage.
Corrects a typo in the 'resets' property name and adds validation to prevent future issues.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver caused by GCC 12. It adds compiler flags to suppress this warning unless extra warnings are enabled.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code and improve readability.

This commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal in io_uring by moving functions to the appropriate file (filetable.c) and adjusting function parameters for better context handling.
This commit splits G1 and G2 decoder nodes in device tree bindings to handle them independently with proper power domain support.
The commit removes an unused macro and integrates its content into another macro to simplify the code structure.
